item.title: Time for another unhinged XL Sunday CeleryDump for the good & bad people of Imgur
item.description:
relatable. greetings online friends
that's extremely impressive
i love horror movies, but some truth here
fly as fuck of course
no need to shame that but... is that ketchup?
we've all been there
wait what
a little terrible music humor for you
mmm hmm
as is tradition
last one made in 2019 apparently
is this a real UFC fighter?
this is a once in a lifetime event
pizza is great
settle down please
it's gonna be Monday in mere hours so yeah. plus lots of increasing interest in moles in general
nailed it
that's my time.. have a great night gang!!
item.dc:creator: CeleryMan7

item.title: While they blamed people wanting a living wage for inflation, oil companies price gouged us, causing 27% of all inflation.
item.description:
The craziest part? Biden tried to pass a bill blocking price gouging at the pump and you'll never believe what happened next... REPUBLICANS BLOCKED IT SO THEY COULD KEEP BLAMING BIDEN FOR INFLATION https://www.thebignewsletter.com/p/an-oil-price-fixing-conspiracy-caused #PriceFixing
BOTTOM LINE: pointing the finger at Biden's administration is clearly the wrong answer. The greedy oil companies are gouging the entire planet.
item.dc:creator: doxydad1

item.title: Cinco de Mayo and the U.S.A.
item.description:
“Why should a (non-Mexican) American celebrate Cinco de Mayo?” Source: https://bsky.app/profile/c0nc0rdance.bsky.social/post/3krqqbizqmg26
Battle of Puebla - https://www.britannica.com/event/Battle-of-Puebla
Book cover of “France and the American Civil War: A Diplomatic History” by Stève Sainlaude - https://uncpress.org/book/9781469649948/france-and-the-american-civil-war/
History.com: “How Cinco de Mayo Helped Prevent a Confederate Victory in the Civil War” https://www.history.com/news/cinco-de-mayo-battle-puebla-civil-war
item.dc:creator: Ratufa

item.title: Woohoo!
item.description: I hope she's at least half as great in person as she is online. I got divorced about a year and a half ago after 24 years of marriage, and a lot has changed since I was last actively dating (late 1990s). Wish me luck. And send memes (she likes them).
item.dc:creator: ElbowDeepInASelfLovingOctopus

item.title: It was great while it lasted... hope my refund request gets through
item.dc:creator: Raoul97533

item.title: Do you know how to make Pysanky?
item.description: Pysanky are an ancient art, made in Ukraine and other Slavic countries for centuries. Many people call them Easter eggs. As a ritual object celebrating the cult of the sun, various forms of the pysanka were also prevalent among the eastern Mediterranean nations 5000 to 3000 years before Christ. The pysanka, a decorated egg which, since pagan times, symbolized nature's rebirth in the spring, was common among all Slavic peoples. For my pysanky I used patterns from Trypilia. Ukraine is the homeland of one of the most ancient civilizations, the Trypillian Civilization, which flourished on its territory about 7,000 years ago.
item.dc:creator: tanbeliaart

item.title: Stressful night
item.description: Had friends over, turns out not such a good idea. One gets too drunk, hits on me, I ask him to leave.he says it's his house, I should leave. I'm going through some stuff about this very thing. It gets physical, I had to choke him out to get him to leave. Old friend hates me now, apparently. I'm glad I was confident (and strong) enough to fight him. Oh yeah it was after everyone else went home. Edit: Thanks everyone for the support and wise words! I know everyone says this but I was not expecting this to hit front page :) you all rock
item.dc:creator: ZippidyDoo

item.title: This is why we choose the bear, we don't know what someone is like until triggered, and that trigger could be as simple as saying no! Lads please try to understand and teach those around you why, that's the only way we'll start to feel safer!

item.title: Woohoo! 2: Electric Boogaloo
item.description: Follow up to https://imgur.com/gallery/5UMy4ds She's very nice, and we had a really nice time. We have made plans to meet again later this week. Thank you, everyone, for the well wishes, good vibes, and for the concerns for me my safety. Y'all really are the best bunch of internet strangers.

item.dc:creator: Pasquinel

item.title: It's a learning curve?
item.description: I was very excited to find out that 3 people had purchased my novel. Today I had an extreme roller coaster moment where someone sent me a picture of the physical copy, a moment of extreme high as I've yet to receive my own copies. And then the moment of descent when it was pointed out that I forgot to add page numbers and the first copies of my book have been dispatched without them. I've fixed the issue, and I guess this is all part of the learning curve but still... Now all that I can do is drink until this story becomes more funny than it is embarrassing. Wish me luck.
item.dc:creator: 0xEF

item.title: Buddy - Rest in Peace my love 2010-2024
item.description:
Baby Buddy
Sweet innocent face
What is this thing?
We were baking Buddy's biscuits!
What's the best sound in the world???
Your absolute all time favorite thing to do!
there was a likely chance he'd get a bit of a smokey
handsome boy
Love, Love, Love that Face!
Garden dog
Your love sustained me
Buddy RIP Dec. 4, 2010 to April 16, 2024
Miss you Buddy! 2010-2024
item.dc:creator: reenaj
